title: Lightly Seasoned Vietnamese Chicken Noodles
description: A flavorful and earthy Vietnamese dish featuring chicken and noodles, perfect for a weeknight dinner, made with a mixture powder and mantecare-emulsify method, and is egg-free friendly, cooked on a tava griddle.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large tava griddle over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the tava griddle and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-6 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the tava griddle and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the tava griddle.
Add the mixture powder to the tava griddle and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the soy sauce, fish sauce, and lime juice to the tava griddle and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cooked noodles to the tava griddle and stir to combine with the sauce.
Add the cooked chicken back to the tava griddle and stir to combine with the noodles and sauce.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the noodles to a serving platter and top with chopped green onions and bean sprouts.
